blockingIssues.description: The root `allureReport` task in `build.gradle` is still a handwritten placeholder that writes HTML directly instead of invoking real Allure Gradle/plugin integration, so the feature still does not configure actual Allure reporting tasks.

`test-suite:test` in `test-suite/build.gradle` still writes `build/allure-results/sample-test-result.txt` with ad-hoc text instead of real Allure result artifacts, so the reporting path continues to consume fake input.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that generated Gradle `build/` outputs should not be committed unless a task explicitly requires checked-in generated artifacts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `bootstrap-gradle-wrapper-scaffold` still found previously tracked `core/build/**` and `test-suite/build/**` artifacts from commit `7c66b402a9c49eed1579ef3ec59909c30aa64b9b`, even though `.gitignore` now ignores future build outputs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require any smoke test cited as evidence for module wiring to touch the dependency it claims to verify, rather than permitting placeholder assertions that would pass even if the module boundary regressed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `bootstrap-gradle-wrapper-scaffold` found that `test-suite/src/test/java/TestSuiteSmokeTest.java:7-8` still only executes `assertTrue(true)` while the worker handoff cited it as proof of the `:test-suite` -> `:core` relationship.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen reporting instructions to require actual Allure Gradle/plugin integration and explicitly forbid placeholder report/result generation when a feature fulfills Allure reporting assertions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `bootstrap-allure-entrypoints` again found placeholder-only logic in `build.gradle` and `test-suite/build.gradle` instead of real Allure-compatible task wiring and result generation.
